questions.question: Explain the difference between FTP active mode and passive mode. Which mode is more commonly used and why?
keyPoints: Active mode: Server initiates data connection to client (PORT command)
Passive mode: Client initiates data connection to server (PASV command)
Passive mode is more firewall-friendly and commonly used
In active, client must accept incoming connections which may be blocked by NAT/firewall

questions.question: Describe the three main components of an electronic mail system and their functions.
keyPoints: Mail User Agent (MUA): Client software for composing, sending, and reading emails
Mail Transfer Agent (MTA): Server software that transfers emails between servers using SMTP
Mail Delivery Agent (MDA): Server software that delivers emails to recipient's mailbox

questions.question: What are the differences between HTTP GET and POST methods? Give appropriate use cases for each.
keyPoints: GET: Data in URL query string, limited length, can be cached, idempotent, for retrieval
POST: Data in message body, larger data capacity, not cached, not idempotent, for submission
Use GET for search queries, reading data; Use POST for form submission, uploading data, transactions

questions.question: Explain why HTTP is called a stateless protocol. How is session management achieved in web applications?
keyPoints: HTTP does not maintain state between requests - each request is independent
No memory of previous transactions or user identity
Session management through: Cookies, URL rewriting, Hidden form fields, Server-side sessions
Cookies store session ID or user data on client side

questions.question: List and briefly explain the functions of any five FTP commands.
keyPoints: GET: Download file from server
PUT: Upload file to server
LIST: List files in directory
CWD: Change working directory
PWD: Print working directory (show current directory)
DELETE: Delete file on server
PASS: Send user password

questions.question: What is the purpose of SMTP? Explain the basic sequence of commands used in SMTP communication.
keyPoints: SMTP is for sending and relaying mail between servers
Sequence: HELO/EHLO (initiate), MAIL FROM (sender), RCPT TO (recipient), DATA (message body), QUIT (terminate)
Uses store-and-forward mechanism
Works on push principle - pushes mail from client to server

questions.question: Compare HTTP and HTTPS protocols with respect to security, port numbers, and use cases.
keyPoints: HTTP: Port 80, no encryption, plain text transfer, for public content
HTTPS: Port 443, TLS/SSL encryption, certificates for authentication, for sensitive data
HTTPS provides confidentiality, integrity, authentication
Used for: Banking, login, e-commerce, any sensitive transaction

questions.question: Explain the working of HTTP with respect to persistent and non-persistent connections. Discuss the advantages of persistent connections.
keyPoints: Non-persistent: New TCP connection for each object, RTT for connection + RTT for request-response, N objects = N connections needed
Persistent (HTTP 1.1): Single TCP connection for multiple objects, pipelining allowed, default in HTTP 1.1
Advantages: Reduced delay (no new connection setup), less server load, better network utilization
Persistent with pipelining: Multiple requests sent without waiting for responses

questions.question: Explain the client-server model of World Wide Web. Describe how a web page is fetched from a server using HTTP protocol.
keyPoints: Client-Server: Browser (client) requests, Server responds with web resources
URL structure: protocol://host:port/path?query
HTTP Request: Method (GET/POST), Headers, Optional body
HTTP Response: Status line, Headers, Message body (HTML)

Process: 1) Resolve IP via DNS 2) Establish TCP connection 3) Send HTTP GET request 4) Server processes and sends response 5) Browser renders content 6) Connection closes (non-persistent) or stays open (persistent)

questions.question: Compare FTP, SMTP, and HTTP protocols under the following heads: (a) Purpose (b) Transport Protocol (c) Connection Type (d) Port Numbers (e) Operation Mode
keyPoints: Purpose: FTP=File transfer, SMTP=Email transfer, HTTP=Web content transfer
Transport: All use TCP for reliable transmission
Connection: FTP uses two channels (control+data), SMTP/HTTP use single channel
Operation: FTP is interactive with command-response, HTTP is request-response, SMTP is store-and-forward mail
FTP maintains state, HTTP is stateless, SMTP transaction-based

questions.question: Explain Telnet protocol. Discuss its working, security issues, and suggest alternatives for secure remote access.
keyPoints: Telnet: Remote terminal protocol on port 23, provides text-based access to remote system
Working: TCP connection to port 23, login credentials transmitted, commands executed on remote system
Security Issues: All data (including passwords) in plain text, vulnerable to eavesdropping, no encryption, susceptible to man-in-middle attacks
Alternatives: SSH (Secure Shell) - encrypted remote access, port 22, provides secure channel
SSH provides: Confidentiality (encryption), Integrity (MAC), Authentication (public key)
